slides.title: The tomato gets *better* when you cook it.
slides.sub: Heat unlocks lycopene, a carotenoid antioxidant your heart and skin will thank you for. Here is why.

slides.title: Meet lycopene
slides.body: Lycopene is the pigment that makes tomatoes red. It is a *carotenoid antioxidant* that helps your cells handle daily stress.

slides.title: Cooking changes everything
slides.body: Raw tomatoes lock lycopene inside tough cell walls. Gentle heat *breaks them open*, so your body absorbs far more of it.

slides.title: A drizzle of olive oil
slides.body: Lycopene is fat soluble. Cooking tomatoes with *a little olive oil* can support how well you take it in.

slides.title: Heart and skin
slides.body: Higher lycopene intake is linked to *better heart health* and skin that handles sun stress more gracefully as you age.

slides.title: Simple ways to get it
slides.body: Tomato sauce, roasted tomatoes, soup and paste are all *rich, easy sources*. Cooked beats raw for this nutrient.

slides.title: Eat well, *track the rest*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the food, sleep, movement and steps behind aging well into one daily Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Turns out the healthiest way to eat a tomato is to cook it. 🍅🌿

Heat unlocks lycopene, a carotenoid antioxidant linked to heart and skin protection, and a little olive oil helps you absorb even more. Save this for your next sauce. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the small habits behind aging well into one daily score → link in bio.
